description: HP 3245A Universal Source: synthesized two-channel voltage/current source generating DC, defined AC waveforms (sine, ramp, square), and arbitrary waveforms, controlled over HP-IB using HP's native English-mnemonic command language. DC voltage range approx -10.25 V to +10.25 V, DC current -0.1 A to +0.1 A. AC voltage output 0.03125 Vpp to 20 Vpp (open circuit), current to 0.2 App. Waveform frequency 0 Hz to 1 MHz. Channel A is addressed as 0/CHANA, channel B as 100/CHANB. Command details are fully documented in the separate HP 3245A Command Reference Manual (03245-90002); this definition is drawn from the command summaries and alphabetical menu command list (Table 6-2) of the Operating & Programming Manual.

identity.description: ID? returns the model number string 'HP3245'. IDN? returns four lines: manufacturer (HEWLETT PACKARD), model (3245), serial number (always 0), and firmware revision code (yyww).

commands.name: identify_model
commands.description: Returns the HP 3245A model number string (HP3245).

commands.name: identify_full
commands.description: Identity query. Returns four lines: manufacturer's name (HEWLETT PACKARD), model number (3245), serial number (always 0), and firmware revision code (yyww, where yy = year - 1960, ww = week).

commands.name: channel_type_query
commands.description: Returns the channel type: '21' for an installed channel or '0' for a non-existent channel. CTYPE? 0 (channel A) always returns 21; CTYPE? 100 (channel B) returns 21 on a two-channel instrument or 0 on a single-channel instrument.

commands.name: revision_query
commands.description: Returns the firmware revision date code in the form 'yyww' (yy = year - 1960, ww = week number).

commands.name: serial_number_query
commands.description: Serial number query.

commands.name: apply_dc_voltage
commands.description: Outputs a DC voltage on the use channel.

commands.name: apply_dc_current
commands.description: Outputs a DC current on the use channel.

commands.name: apply_triggered_dc_voltage
commands.description: Outputs successive triggered DC voltages from a defined memory array, one value output per trigger event (set by TRIGIN).

commands.name: apply_triggered_dc_current
commands.description: Outputs successive triggered DC currents from a defined memory array, one value output per trigger event (set by TRIGIN).

commands.name: apply_sine_voltage
commands.description: Outputs a sine wave voltage (peak-to-peak) on the use channel.

commands.name: apply_sine_current
commands.description: Outputs a sine wave current (peak-to-peak) on the use channel.

commands.name: apply_ramp_voltage
commands.description: Outputs a ramp wave voltage (peak-to-peak) on the use channel.

commands.name: apply_ramp_current
commands.description: Outputs a ramp wave current (peak-to-peak) on the use channel.

commands.name: apply_square_voltage
commands.description: Outputs a square wave voltage (peak-to-peak) on the use channel.

commands.name: apply_square_current
commands.description: Outputs a square wave current (peak-to-peak) on the use channel.

commands.name: apply_arbitrary_voltage
commands.description: Outputs an arbitrary (user-defined or precomputed) voltage waveform from an array.

commands.name: apply_arbitrary_current
commands.description: Outputs an arbitrary (user-defined or precomputed) current waveform from an array.

commands.name: apply_query
commands.description: Queries the current output function of the use channel.

commands.name: output_value_query
commands.description: Returns the present output value (DC level or AC peak-to-peak amplitude) of the use channel.

commands.name: frequency
commands.description: Sets or queries the waveform frequency. For dual-frequency triggered mode two values may be given (FREQ f1,f2).

commands.name: dc_offset
commands.description: Sets or queries the DC offset voltage applied to an AC/arbitrary waveform. Limited to about 50% of the selected range.

commands.name: duty_cycle
commands.description: Sets or queries the duty cycle (percentage) of square/ramp waveforms.

commands.name: phase_angle
commands.description: Sets or queries the phase angle of the waveform in degrees.

commands.name: use_channel
commands.description: Selects the use channel for subsequent commands. Channel A = 0 or CHANA, channel B = 100 or CHANB.

commands.name: output_terminal
commands.description: Selects or queries the output terminal (FRONT or REAR). Power-on is FRONT.

commands.name: output_impedance
commands.description: Sets or queries the output impedance (0 ohm or 50 ohm). Power-on is 0 ohm.

commands.name: output_range
commands.description: Sets or queries the fixed output range (max_output). Omitting the parameter selects autorange.

commands.name: autorange
commands.description: Enables or queries autorange mode. Power-on is autorange ON.

commands.name: dc_resolution
commands.description: Sets or queries the DC resolution mode (high or low resolution). Power-on is high-resolution.

commands.name: output_delay
commands.description: Sets or queries the output settling delay time. Power-on default 0.04 seconds.

commands.name: trigger_input_source
commands.description: Sets or queries the trigger input source/event that triggers an output or waveform. SGL is a software single trigger; HIGH/LOW set software trigger levels; EXT uses the external Trigger I/O port. Power-on is HIGH.

parameters.name: event
parameters.range: HIGH, LOW, SGL, EXT, HOLD, TBn

commands.name: trigger_mode
commands.description: Sets or queries the waveform trigger mode. OFF = no triggering, ARMWF = armed/synchronized, GATED = gated, DUALFR = dual-frequency. Power-on is OFF (no effect on DCV/DCI).

commands.name: trigger_output
commands.description: Sets or queries the trigger output destination. EXT routes the channel trigger signal to the channel Trigger (I/O) port.

commands.name: pulse_trigger_bus
commands.description: Pulses the internal trigger bus.

commands.name: drive_trigger_bus
commands.description: Sets or queries the source driving trigger bus n. Power-on is Off.

commands.name: trigger_bus_query
commands.description: Reads the current logic level of trigger bus n.

commands.name: ref_frequency_input
commands.description: Sets or queries the reference frequency input source (INT or EXT). Power-on is INT.

commands.name: ref_frequency_output
commands.description: Sets or queries the reference frequency output destination (INT or EXT). Power-on is EXT.

commands.name: synchronized_output_mode
commands.description: Generates synchronized (phase-locked) outputs across channels.

commands.name: sync_out
commands.description: Sets or queries the sync-out signal destination. Power-on is Off.

commands.name: fast_amplitude_sweep
commands.description: Performs a fast amplitude/offset change (fast amplitude sweep) for high-speed level changes.

commands.name: fast_frequency_sweep
commands.description: Performs a fast frequency sweep for high-speed frequency changes.

commands.name: define_real_array
commands.description: Defines (declares) a REAL (floating-point) array in instrument memory.

commands.name: dim_real_array
commands.description: Defines (dimensions) a REAL array in instrument memory.

commands.name: define_integer_array
commands.description: Defines an INTEGER array (values rounded to -32768..+32767) in instrument memory.

commands.name: fill_array
commands.description: Fills an array with a list of values.

commands.name: fill_sine_array
commands.description: Fills an array with a precomputed sine waveform.

commands.name: fill_ramp_array
commands.description: Fills an array with a precomputed ramp waveform.

commands.name: fill_arbitrary_array
commands.description: Fills an array with an arbitrary (user-defined) waveform.

commands.name: define_subroutine
commands.description: Begins definition of a named subroutine.

commands.name: end_subroutine
commands.description: Ends a subroutine definition.

commands.name: call_subroutine
commands.description: Calls (executes) a stored subroutine.

commands.name: run_subroutine
commands.description: Runs a stored subroutine.

commands.name: list_subroutine
commands.description: Lists the contents of a stored subroutine.

commands.name: delete_subroutine
commands.description: Deletes a stored subroutine.

commands.name: abort_subroutine
commands.description: Aborts a running subroutine.

commands.name: compress_subroutine
commands.description: Compresses a stored subroutine to reclaim memory.

commands.name: pause_subroutine
commands.description: Pauses a running subroutine.

commands.name: pause_query
commands.description: Returns whether a subroutine is currently paused.

commands.name: continue_subroutine
commands.description: Continues a paused subroutine.

commands.name: single_step_subroutine
commands.description: Single-steps a paused subroutine.

commands.name: running_query
commands.description: Returns whether a subroutine is currently running.

commands.name: catalog
commands.description: Catalogs the subroutines, arrays, and variables stored in instrument memory.

commands.name: memory_available_query
commands.description: Returns the amount of available instrument memory.

commands.name: size_query
commands.description: Returns the size of a named array.

commands.name: scratch
commands.description: Deletes all stored subroutines, variables, and arrays from instrument memory.

commands.name: store_state
commands.description: Stores the complete HP 3245A instrument state into a numbered register.

commands.name: store_channel_a_state
commands.description: Stores the channel A state into a numbered register.

commands.name: store_channel_b_state
commands.description: Stores the channel B state into a numbered register.

commands.name: recall_state
commands.description: Recalls a complete HP 3245A instrument state from a numbered register.

commands.name: recall_channel_a_state
commands.description: Recalls the channel A state from a numbered register.

commands.name: recall_channel_b_state
commands.description: Recalls the channel B state from a numbered register.

commands.name: send_state
commands.description: Sends a complete HP 3245A learn-string state to the instrument.

commands.name: copy_channel_a_to_b
commands.description: Copies the channel A state to channel B.

commands.name: copy_channel_b_to_a
commands.description: Copies the channel B state to channel A.

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Resets the HP 3245A (or a specified channel) to its known power-on state. RESET with no parameter resets the entire instrument. RST is an accepted synonym.

commands.name: channel_reset
commands.description: Resets the use channel to its known state.

commands.name: clear
commands.description: Places the HP 3245A in the same state as the HP-IB device clear (CLEAR) command. CLEAR is an accepted synonym.

commands.name: confidence_test
commands.description: Performs a confidence self-test on the HP 3245A or a specified channel. Sets the output to 0 V on the tested channel and returns 'PASS' or 'FAIL'.

commands.name: device_self_test
commands.description: Performs the device self-test.

commands.name: fixtured_self_test
commands.description: Performs the fixtured self-test.

commands.name: channel_adjustment
commands.description: Performs a channel adjustment (calibration) operation.

commands.name: calibration_security
commands.description: Sets or controls the calibration security (protection).

commands.name: cal_jumper_query
commands.description: Reads the calibration enable jumper setting.

commands.name: calibration_number_query
commands.description: Returns the calibration number (count of calibrations performed).

commands.name: calibration_data_query
commands.description: Reads stored calibration data.

commands.name: status_word_query
commands.description: Returns the 16-bit status word. Bits are set when corresponding conditions occur.

commands.name: status_byte_query
commands.description: Returns the status byte.

commands.name: service_request_enable
commands.description: Sets or queries the service request (SRQ) enable mask. Bits set in the RQS mask allow corresponding status conditions to generate an SRQ.

commands.name: programmed_service_request
commands.description: Generates a programmed (front panel) service request.

commands.name: power_on_srq
commands.description: Enables or disables the power-on service request.

commands.name: error_string_query
commands.description: Returns the next error as an error number and descriptive string (e.g. 0,"NO ERROR").

commands.name: monitor_conditions
commands.description: Sets the front panel monitor mode. MON HP-IB displays the latest command received over HP-IB.

commands.name: output_format
commands.description: Selects the numeric output data format: ASCII (comma-separated, power-on default) or BINARY (16-bit integer / 64-bit IEEE-754).

commands.name: block_output_mode
commands.description: Determines whether the IEEE-728 Block A header is sent before each binary output.

commands.name: input_buffer
commands.description: Enables or disables input buffering. With INBUF ON commands are accepted into the buffer immediately; with INBUF OFF commands are accepted only when the instrument is not busy.

commands.name: output_buffer
commands.description: Enables or controls output buffering.

commands.name: clear_output_buffer
commands.description: Clears the output buffer.

commands.name: ready_query
commands.description: Returns whether the instrument is ready to accept a new command (used with input buffering).

commands.name: wait
commands.description: Pauses command execution for the specified time in seconds.

commands.name: hpib_address
commands.description: Sets or queries the HP-IB bus address. Factory default is 09.

commands.name: go_to_local
commands.description: Returns the instrument to local (front panel) control.

commands.name: remote
commands.description: Places the instrument in remote control state.

commands.name: set_time
commands.description: Sets the internal real-time clock.

commands.name: read_time
commands.description: Reads the internal real-time clock.

commands.name: beep
commands.description: Sounds the instrument beeper.

commands.name: help
commands.description: Displays front panel command syntax help for an optional topic.

commands.name: demo
commands.description: Runs the built-in HP 3245A demonstration.
